methodology.backtesting: Walk-forward validation. For each ticker, we train on 252 days of price history, then test predictions on the next 21 days. We step forward 7 days and repeat. This avoids look-ahead bias — the model never sees future data during training.
methodology.directional_accuracy: We predict UP (if Monte Carlo win rate > 55%), DOWN (< 45%), or NEUTRAL. DA measures how often UP/DOWN predictions are correct, excluding neutrals.
methodology.monte_carlo: We run 30 seeds × 750 simulated price paths using historical volatility. The average win rate across seeds feeds into the composite score.
methodology.composite_score: Average of 4 components: (1) data completeness — how much price/fundamental data was available, (2) scorer agreement — how much the 6 scorers agree, (3) Monte Carlo win rate, (4) historical calibration — past accuracy on this ticker. Range 0-1.
methodology.forward_testing: Each day at 9:35am ET, we run the full pipeline on 39 tickers and store predictions. After 21 calendar days, we fetch the actual price and record whether we were right.
methodology.tiers: Significant edge: DA >= 60% with 10+ windows. Candidate: DA >= 55% with 5+ windows. Weak: below threshold.

data_sources.price_ohlcv: yfinance Python library. Free, no API key required. Daily OHLCV going back to 2018.
data_sources.fundamentals: yfinance Ticker.info. Provides P/E ratio, P/B ratio, ROE, debt-to-equity, gross margin, revenue growth, earnings growth, market cap, FCF yield.
data_sources.macro: FRED API (St. Louis Fed). Currently NOT active — no API key configured. Macro scorer defaults to 50/100. To activate: set FRED_API_KEY environment variable.
data_sources.backtest_db: SQLite database at financial-analysis-agent/data/financial_analysis.db. Contains 1,389,731 backtest windows across 434 tickers and 16 industries.

scorers.momentum: 0-100. Based on RSI (14), MACD histogram, price vs 50/200-day moving averages, volume ratio. High score = strong upward momentum.
scorers.value: 0-100. Based on P/E ratio, P/B ratio, P/S ratio, FCF yield. High score = stock is cheap relative to fundamentals.
scorers.growth: 0-100. Based on revenue growth YoY, earnings growth YoY, gross margin. High score = strong and accelerating growth.
scorers.mean_reversion: 0-100. Based on Bollinger Band position, distance from 200-day MA, RSI extremes. High score = stock is oversold and likely to bounce.
scorers.macro: 0-100. Based on yield curve (10Y-2Y spread), 10-year treasury rate, unemployment rate. CURRENTLY INACTIVE — defaults to 50.
scorers.quality: 0-100. Based on ROE, operating margin, debt-to-equity, market cap. High score = high-quality business.

known_limitations: Macro scorer is inactive (no FRED API key). All macro scores are 50/100.
Fundamental data (P/E, ROE etc) is fetched live, not point-in-time. This may introduce slight look-ahead bias in fundamental scorers.
Only GE passes all statistical gates (bootstrap CI + permutation test). All other tickers are in training mode.
Forward-test predictions have 21-day horizon. No predictions have resolved yet — accuracy is based on backtest only.
Very short (1-2 day) and short (2-5 day) intervals produce only neutral signals — Monte Carlo win rates cluster too tightly around 50% to breach the threshold.
